confidence_adjustments.signal_type: 52-week low breach for technically weak stocks (consumer, FII-selling)
confidence_adjustments.reason: P001 (SWIGGY breach ₹260) RIGHT — breached on Day 2 after prediction despite intraday bounce (+2.68% on day of breach, but still closed below 52-week low). FII selling pressure overwhelms technical support faster than expected. Treat breach as base case (high conviction), not tail risk.

P006 (Nifty bounce Apr 2) RIGHT — Nifty closed 22,713.10 vs target >22,600 (hit by +113 pts). March 30 forced selling (Nifty -2.14%) exhausted by April 2 (+1.71% bounce). Historically reliable seasonal pattern (70%+ success). Elevate to HIGH confidence (4/5).

confidence_adjustments.signal_type: Geopolitical ceasefire rumors signaled via gold correction
confidence_adjustments.reason: P008 trajectory WRONG — Gold fell from ATH $5,595 to $4,520 on reported 15-point ceasefire proposal (March 26), suggesting market priced in de-escalation. But Iran formally rejected ceasefire March 25; Trump escalated April 2. Brent still $107-111, not <$100. Market priced in ceasefire that did not materialize. Do NOT use ceasefire rumors for <4-week predictions without formal signed agreement.

P007 (SWIGGY no breach, will hold >₹270) WRONG — SWIGGY breached ₹260 within 48 hours despite +2.68% intraday bounce on day of breach. Created as counter-prediction to P001 on same day. Without confirmed positive earnings catalyst, institutional accumulation, or technical reversal setup, betting against 52-week low breach in FII-selling environment is very low-probability (confidence 1).

Government ₹79,000 Cr defence procurement confirmed. Maintain CRITICAL conviction (5/5) on defence sector upside. Valuations stretched (50.6x PE) but geopolitical premium justified.
